subject: Chemistry
title: The Pharaoh's Serpent
description: Learn The Pharaoh's Serpent in one minute.

hook.text: Light mercury thiocyanate and a dark, snake-like column erupts from the tiny pellet, growing like a living thing.

buildup.text: The heat triggers decomposition. Carbon nitride ash expands dramatically as gases push it outward.

discovery.text: The 'serpent' is a foamy carbon structure inflated by nitrogen and sulfur gases escaping the reaction.

twist.text: This used to be sold as a children's firework in the 1800s—until people realized mercury fumes are toxic.

climax.text: Modern versions use baking soda and sugar. Safer, but the original remains chemistry's creepiest demo.

punchline.text: A pellet becomes a dark serpent. Chemistry's spookiest trick.

quiz.question: Why was the original Pharaoh's Serpent firework banned?
options: Mercury thiocyanate produces toxic mercury fumes
The snake was too realistic and scared people
